text:
Implementation is done, but I want to flag honestly: the verification run was blocked — running `node` requires your approval in this environment, so I have no execution evidence to point to. The code itself is the canonical Wagner–Fischer dynamic-programming edit distance, not a special-cased solution.

What `editDistance(a, b)` in `edit_distance.js` does:
- Builds the DP table where `cost[i][j]` is the minimum cost to turn the first `i` characters of `a` into the first `j` characters of `b`, kept to two rolling rows for O(min memory).
- Base rows: turning `""` into `b[0..j]` costs `j` insertions; turning `a[0..i]` into `""` costs `i` deletions.
- Each cell takes the cheapest of: delete from `a` (`+1`), insert from `b` (`+1`), or match/substitute (`+0` if characters equal, else `+2`).

If you approve `node`, this command checks it against an independent brute-force reference on 5000 random strings plus spot cases:

```bash
node -e 'const ed=require("./edit_distance");function ref(a,b){if(!a.length)return b.length;if(!b.length)return a.length;const s=a[0]===b[0]?0:2;return Math.min(ref(a.slice(1),b)+1,ref(a,b.slice(1))+1,ref(a.slice(1),b.slice(1))+s)}const r=()=>Array.from({length:(Math.random()*6)|0},()=>String.fromCharCode(97+(Math.random()*3|0))).join("");let f=0;for(let i=0;i<5000;i++){const a=r(),b=r();if(ed(a,b)!==ref(a,b)){console.log("MISMATCH",a,b);f++}}console.log(f?"FAIL "+f:"ALL MATCH")'
```
